purpose: Evidence-first reference library for Base agent/product tokens. These are patterns and comparison anchors, not investment recommendations.

caseTypes.real_build: Website and GitHub show a usable product path, maintained repo, docs, SDK/API or app evidence.
caseTypes.promising_but_unproven: Narrative and links exist, but product usage or repo proof is incomplete.
caseTypes.front_end_shell: Polished website exists, but demo is simulated or static and GitHub/backend proof is weak.
caseTypes.repo_mismatch: GitHub found by search only, or repo does not match the token/product claim.
caseTypes.short_lived_launch: Launchpad/social token with weak product surface; may trade, but evidence does not support a product claim.

strongWebsite: live app route, dashboard, login, wallet action, or real user flow
docs that point to concrete contracts, SDK functions, API endpoints, changelog, or deployment addresses
backend/API calls visible in frontend or public docs
clear product screenshots or demo tied to real state, not only a landing page
official links are internally consistent across website, X, docs, and GitHub
weakWebsite: simulated demo or teaser explicitly says it is not live
only landing page sections with generic AI/agent/workflow words
footer links, terms, docs, GitHub, or social links are missing, dead, or template-like
claimed products are mostly 'building', 'coming soon', or roadmap
strongGithub: website or official X explicitly links the repo/org
repo has recent commits, real code structure, README, docs, examples, package files, tests, issues, or releases
repo names align with the product surface, for example app, contracts, sdk, docs, indexer, mcp
contracts or SDK addresses match the token/site claims
multiple repos in the org tell a coherent product story
weakGithub: GitHub URL returns 404, private, deleted, or empty
repo is only a same-name search result and not linked by official sources
repo has only generated boilerplate, no meaningful commits, or stale last push
repo content does not match the token/project narrative
no contracts, app, SDK, docs, examples, or deployment references

comparisonCases.name: Gitbank
comparisonCases.baseLaunchContext: Base product/devtool token with website, docs, X and GitHub org discoverable from token metadata.
websiteEvidence: project website and docs discoverable from DEX Screener
site describes a concrete GitHub-linked product surface
githubEvidence: related repos such as CLI, SDK, app, contracts, playground form a coherent product map
recent updates support an active builder story
watchFor: whether real users adopt the product beyond launch attention
whether contracts and docs remain consistent with the token story
comparisonCases.heuristic: Use as a positive comparison for devtool tokens: official org + multiple coherent repos + docs/app beats pure narrative wording.

comparisonCases.name: Tessera
comparisonCases.baseLaunchContext: AI agent credit/USDC line narrative with polished website and X link, but weak public repo proof at inspection time.
websiteEvidence: polished website with Products, Docs, Demo, SDK routes
many roadmap/building claims but limited proof of live credit usage
githubEvidence: official site referenced a GitHub path that was inaccessible/404 at inspection time
same-name GitHub search results should not be treated as official
watchFor: repo becomes public and matches contracts/SDK claims
real draw/repay/settlement events appear and are linked from docs
official X demonstrates product usage instead of only narrative
comparisonCases.heuristic: Use as a warning comparison: a strong Base-native story plus good design is not enough without repo/backend/onchain usage proof.

comparisonCases.name: Clanker-style social launches
comparisonCases.baseLaunchContext: Tokens launched from social/farcaster flow may have momentum without a product.
websiteEvidence: often no dedicated website or only launchpad/social page
githubEvidence: usually no official repo; same-name search should be ignored unless official account links it
watchFor: whether original dev or related builder recognizes it
whether the story survives beyond first-day social flow
comparisonCases.heuristic: Do not penalize every Clanker token for lacking GitHub, but do not call it a product token without product evidence.

comparisonCases.name: Virtuals-style agent launches
comparisonCases.baseLaunchContext: Agent tokens may have richer pages but still need API/demo/revenue evidence.
websiteEvidence: look for runnable demo, API connection, agent logs, revenue/use cases, team or ecosystem integrations
generic agent biography is weak evidence
githubEvidence: repo is a strong plus only when it contains agent code, integrations, evals, API server, or SDK usage
no repo is acceptable for some closed teams, but then product usage evidence must be stronger
watchFor: actual agent behavior, not just a character page
API calls, integrations, autonomous actions, or paying users
comparisonCases.heuristic: For AI agent tokens, demand behavioral proof: demo/API/logs/revenue matter more than agent lore.

comparisonCases.name: Flaunch / Zora creator mechanisms
comparisonCases.baseLaunchContext: Creator/mechanism tokens should be judged by whether the mechanism creates demand, not by devtool standards.
websiteEvidence: look for creator fee mechanics, hooks, content distribution, redemption, or user flow
landing pages with mechanism buzzwords but no usage path are weak
githubEvidence: same-name repo search is weak unless linked by official pages
watchFor: whether creator/community actually uses the mechanism
comparisonCases.heuristic: For mechanism tokens, the product evidence is the working mechanism and usage loop, not the beauty of the site.

reportingRules: Lead with product evidence grade before narrative score.
Treat official website-linked GitHub as strong; same-name GitHub search as weak.
Call out simulated demos, dead GitHub links, template footers, and missing backend signals in plain language.
Compare the project to the closest case type: real_build, front_end_shell, repo_mismatch, short_lived_launch, or promising_but_unproven.
Do not turn a hot narrative into a high product judgment without website/GitHub/app evidence.
